mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so month-over-month declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS month-over-month declines than after flat or rising readings, or if the sign of the effect flips in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when unemployment is accelerating.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the triggered signal loses directional accuracy in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth in RSAFS is accompanied by firmer GDP growth in GDPC1, the combination signals broad final-demand acceleration that should lift equities over the next quarter through improved revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS and GDPC1 both trend higher does not outperform either series alone for predicting positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO together with a rebound in HOUST signals cyclical production and construction momentum, which should support cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous rises in INDPRO and HOUST do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the joint signal fails to outperform single-proxy versions in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound implies real rates are falling and policy is constrained, which supports risk assets over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and CPIAUCSL inflation rises versus the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the low-rate inflation-up episodes do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than matched controls, or if the sign becomes negative once FEDFUNDS is above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Stable PAYEMS growth with falling UNRATE signals resilient payroll expansion and labor absorption, which should support equity returns over the next 5 trading days through improved cyclical confidence.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases and UNRATE falls over the latest monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if monthly payroll gains combined with falling unemployment fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction effect is not robust across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained slowdown in RSAFS indicates weakening consumption demand and lower near-term revenue growth, so a downward change in retail sales should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns negative on a 3-month rolling basis
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in most triggered cases, or if the signal loses sign consistency after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST also rises, industrial and housing capacity expansion indicates synchronized real activity that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; the effect fails when either series is flat or contracting.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upward moves in INDPRO and HOUST do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the interaction disappears during periods when either proxy is not trending up.
